free_text: Nuclear fragments in the interactions of carbon
nuclei with a beryllium target at the energy of 0.95
GeV per nucleon
English translation
Russian original
TWA heavy-ion accelerator
The FRAGM facility was used.
Scintillation counters were used to measure
ionization losses and the time of flight. A telescope
formed by three scintillation counters and directed
immediately to the accelerator target at an angle of
2 degrees with respect to the ion beam served as a
monitor.
Thin 50 mu-m beryllium target was used.
Total systematic error in the double-
differential cross sections
Statistical error
Entry was approved by Dr. A.P. Krutenkova
